subject: Physics
overall_learning_objectives: Explain Earth's rotation and revolution using models
Investigate how shadow length changes during the day
Analyze the phases of the Moon and the lunar cycle
Identify constellations and their seasonal movement
Explain solar and lunar eclipses using astronomical alignment
Understand ancient and modern methods of timekeeping
Relate sky observations to calendar systems
Develop observation and analytical skills in astronomy

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Time Through Astronomical Cycles
topics_covered: Role of Sun and Moon in timekeeping
student_activities: Discuss natural cycles such as sunrise and sunset
Explain how ancient people estimated time

lesson_plan.title: Earth’s Rotation and Day-Night Cycle
student_activities: Rotate globe to simulate day-night cycle

lesson_plan.title: Shadow Investigation and Sundial
student_activities: Measure shadow length at different times

lesson_plan.title: Phases of the Moon and Lunar Cycle
teaching_strategy: Model demonstration using torch and ball
student_activities: Track Moon phases for several days

lesson_plan.title: Constellations and Star Movement
topics_covered: Examples such as Orion and Ursa Major

lesson_plan.title: Solar and Lunar Eclipses
topics_covered: Alignment of Sun, Earth, and Moon

lesson_plan.title: Astronomy and Calendar Systems
student_activities: Explain how lunar phases influence months

lesson_plan.title: Applications and Chapter Evaluation
topics_covered: Importance of astronomy in navigation and timekeeping
student_activities: Explain how sky observation helps measure time

teacher_tip: Encourage students to observe the Moon phases and star patterns over several days. Real sky observations strengthen understanding of astronomical timekeeping concepts.
